---
title: "r5r Data Prevalidation "
---

## 1. Load Packages

```{r}
# Load packages
library(r5r)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(tmap)
```

## 2. Data Loading and Pre-processing

```{r}
# Load London LSOA shapefile
lsoa_london <- st_read("lsoa_london/lsoa_london.shp")
```

```{r}
# Load Greenspace Access Points
greenspace_accesspoint_london <- st_read("OS-Open-Greenspace-GB/GB_AccessPoint/GB_AccessPoint.shp") %>%
  # Ensure consistent CRS
  st_transform(st_crs(lsoa_london)) %>%
  # Filter to London only
  .[st_intersects(., st_union(lsoa_london), sparse = FALSE), ]
```

```{r}
# filter to all Camden LSOAs
camden_lsoa <- lsoa_london %>%
  filter(grepl("Camden", LSOA21NM))

# check how many LSOAs Camden has
nrow(camden_lsoa)
```

## 3. Create Origin and Destinations

### Origin

```{r}
sf_use_s2(FALSE)

# create origin points
origins <- st_centroid(camden_lsoa) %>%
  # reproject
  st_transform(4326) %>%
  # create new column
  mutate(
    id  = LSOA21CD,
    lon = unlist(map(geometry,1)),
    lat = unlist(map(geometry,2))
  )
# remove geometry column
origins$geometry <- NULL
# only keep id, lon and lat
origins <- origins %>%
  select(id, lon, lat)

head(origins, 5)
```

### Destination

Testing on Camden borough only, so filter greenspace access points to Camden only first:

```{r}
greenspace_accesspoint_camden <- greenspace_accesspoint_london[
  st_intersects(greenspace_accesspoint_london, st_union(camden_lsoa), sparse = FALSE), ]

nrow(greenspace_accesspoint_camden)
```

Now set destination:

```{r}
destinations <- greenspace_accesspoint_camden %>%
  st_transform(4326) %>%
  mutate(
    id  = as.character(row_number()),
    lon = unlist(map(geometry, 1)),
    lat = unlist(map(geometry, 2))
  )

destinations$geometry <- NULL

destinations <- destinations %>%
  select(id, lon, lat)

head(destinations, 5)
cat("Number of destination access points:", nrow(destinations), "\n")
```

## 4. r5r

```{r}
# increase Java memory
options(java.parameters = "-Xmx6G")

# load libraries
library(r5r)

#path <- system.file("r5r_path", package = "r5r")
r5r_core <- r5r::build_network(data_path = "r5r_data/r5r_london_data")

# used setup_r5() previously and got the following: 
# Warning: ! `setup_r5()` is being deprecated in *r5r* v2.3.0 and will be removed in a future release. ℹ Please use `r5r::build_network()` instead.
```

Select routing parameters (travel times)

```{r}
# routing paramaters
# travel by public transport only

mode_transit <- c("TRANSIT")

# maximum trip durations to be 1 hour (60 min)
max_trip_duration <- 60 

# can change to just a day (no time) and it will return min time of the entire day 
# here I chose 29/06/2025 and 12 p.m. for testing purposes
departure_datetime <- as.POSIXct("14-07-2026 12:00:00", 
                                 format = "%d-%m-%Y %H:%M:%S",
                                 tz = "Europe/London")

# estimating the travel time
TTM_Camden_PT <- travel_time_matrix(r5r_core,
                          origins = origins,
                          destinations = destinations,
                          mode = mode_transit,
                          departure_datetime = departure_datetime,
                          max_trip_duration = max_trip_duration)
```

```{r}
nrow(TTM_Camden_PT)
head(TTM_Camden_PT)
summary(TTM_Camden_PT$travel_time_p50)
```

```{r}
# routing paramaters
# travel by walking only 

mode_walk <- c("WALK")

max_trip_duration <- 60

departure_datetime <- as.POSIXct("14-07-2026 12:00:00", 
                                 format = "%d-%m-%Y %H:%M:%S",
                                 tz = "Europe/London")

# estimating the travel time
TTM_Camden_walk <- travel_time_matrix(r5r_core,
                          origins = origins,
                          destinations = destinations,
                          mode = mode_walk,
                          departure_datetime = departure_datetime,
                          max_trip_duration = max_trip_duration)
```

```{r}
nrow(TTM_Camden_walk)
head(TTM_Camden_walk)
summary(TTM_Camden_walk$travel_time_p50)
```

```{r}
# create one test destination far away - Hyde Park corner
test_destination <- data.frame(
  id  = "hyde_park",
  lon = -0.1507,
  lat = 51.5027
)

# run transit vs walk to this one far destination
test_transit <- travel_time_matrix(r5r_core,
                          origins = origins,
                          destinations = test_destination,
                          mode = c("WALK", "TRANSIT"),
                          departure_datetime = departure_datetime,
                          max_trip_duration = 60,
                          time_window = 10)

test_walk <- travel_time_matrix(r5r_core,
                          origins = origins,
                          destinations = test_destination,
                          mode = "WALK",
                          departure_datetime = departure_datetime,
                          max_trip_duration = 60)

# compare
summary(test_transit$travel_time_p50)
summary(test_walk$travel_time_p50)
```
